strengths: Successfully completed all required phases including suture introduction and target passes
Demonstrated proper extracorporeal knot tying technique with consistent throw sequences
Achieved adequate slit closure without tissue avulsion or excessive tension
weaknesses: Completion time of 190 seconds significantly exceeds intermediate target of 112 seconds
Camera positioning and visual assessment challenges affected precision verification
Inconsistent pacing between phases suggests room for workflow optimization

drills.name: Mark-to-mark needle path drill
drills.description: Practice 15 passes through both target marks with deliberate accuracy verification before any knot tying to improve precision and speed.
drills.targets_weakness: Camera positioning and visual assessment challenges affected precision verification

drills.name: Knot-pusher sequencing drill
drills.description: Perform 12 three-throw extracorporeal sequences with deliberate pusher advancement, focusing on smooth transitions between throws.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent pacing between phases suggests room for workflow optimization

drills.name: Slit-closure check drill
drills.description: Practice tightening knots just enough to close the slit without avulsion, then visually inspect closure quality before cutting.
drills.targets_weakness: Completion time significantly exceeds intermediate target

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid technical foundation into faster, more fluid execution. Target sub-120 second completion times while maintaining your current accuracy standards.
